summary.intent: Fix git-sync materialized push failures against Cloudflare-Artifacts-class HTTP endpoints, which were consistently dying at ~7.9 KB with 'use of closed network connection'.
summary.outcome: Root cause identified as Cloudflare's idle-write timeout triggered by go-git's delta selection phase stalling the HTTP body mid-stream. Fixed by pre-running delta selection before opening the POST body using a new upstream go-git API (WithObjectSelector), which was contributed and merged as go-git PR #2142. git-sync PR #64 opened with full fix plus HTTP diagnostics, keep-alives fix, and pack-encode progress. Credential-reject-on-401 feature started on a separate branch.

repo: Materialized push strategy previously used io.Pipe between go-git encoder and HTTP body — pack bytes flowed directly to wire with chunked encoding, but delta selection caused a 50s+ stall mid-stream.
Relay/bootstrap paths (PushPack) stream source bytes continuously from GitHub and are unaffected by the idle-write issue; the stall is specific to materialized strategy's local encoding.
Progress output uses sessionStderr → setTransient pattern: write strings ending with \r to the ProgressWriter to get transient/overwriting lines on stderr.
GITSYNC_HTTP_TRACE=1 env var pattern mirrors GITSYNC_FORCE_CHUNKED=1 — env-var-gated debug/experiment flags are the repo's convention for diagnostics.
GitCredentialFillCommand / GitCredentialRejectCommand are function-variable hooks (not hard-coded exec calls) to enable testability without shelling out in tests.
Entire-Checkpoint trailers are injected by the prepare-commit-msg hook; the hook does NOT re-fire during git rebase --continue, so amended commits lose the trailer.
go-git's Encoder.Encode is internally two phases: ObjectsToPack (slow, no output) then encode (fast, streaming). These were not previously separable via the public API.
Commit message convention is '<area>: <lowercase imperative verb> <description>' with subject under ~70 chars; linter enforces wrapcheck for error wrapping.

code.finding: countingWriter needed atomic.Int64 because the progress ticker goroutine reads the counter concurrently with the encoder writing — flagged by efficiency review.
sessionStderr had a data race: concurrent writes from the progress ticker goroutine and the main goroutine required a mutex, caught by PR review comment.
lookupGitCredential calls 'git credential fill' to obtain credentials but never calls 'git credential approve' or 'git credential reject', so stale tokens persist in the helper across runs.
SpooledBody exposed unexported size/path fields directly to PostRPCStreamBody; encapsulated via applyTo(req) method during simplify pass.

Encoder.Encode mutates ObjectToPack entries (Offset, WantWrite, cycle recovery via restoreOriginal) during the write phase, making any pre-built []*ObjectToPack single-use — reusing across two encoders produces silently broken packs with mismatched headers/footers and wrong hashes.

ObjectsToPack parallelizes delta walking with goroutines per object type — this is why the phase must complete before writing starts; sorting and parallel walks require the full object list in memory before any bytes flow.

workflow: Isolate causal variables with a minimal experiment flag (GITSYNC_FORCE_CHUNKED=1) rather than reasoning from first principles — the Content-Length hypothesis was plausible but wrong; the experiment proved it in minutes.
When interactive-rebasing to amend a non-HEAD commit, stash unrelated working-tree changes first to avoid them appearing staged mid-rebase.
force-with-lease over --force when updating a PR branch: fails safely if someone else pushed in the meantime.
For upstream OSS contributions: export the internal phase boundary rather than adding a new high-level API — lower maintenance burden and easier to get accepted.
When a go module dep is pinned to an unreleased commit (pseudo-version), document the merge commit hash explicitly in the PR so reviewers know to bump when a tag drops.
Squash to one commit before upstream PR merge; preserve Co-authored-by trailers for collaborators who committed directly to the branch.

friction: Initial diagnosis was wrong — framed as 'Cloudflare rejects chunked encoding' when the actual cause was Cloudflare's idle-write timeout during delta selection; commit messages were amended after the experiment clarified the root cause.
GITSYNC_FORCE_CHUNKED=1 experiment unexpectedly still sent Content-Length (spooled body had already computed size), requiring a second look at the code path to understand why.
Interactive rebase dropped Entire-Checkpoint trailers from two commits (prepare-commit-msg hook doesn't re-fire on rebase --continue); caught only at lint-check time.
go-git hadn't cut a new alpha tag after PR #2142 merged, requiring a pseudo-version pin in go.mod rather than a clean tagged release.
PR #64 had lint failures post-push: wrapcheck violation on a bare error return and a data race in sessionStderr missed during local testing.
PrecomputedSelector was added to go-git then removed after maintainer feedback across multiple PR iterations, requiring force-push and squash.
open_items: go-git dependency pinned to pseudo-version (merge commit 590487407c38); needs bump to next tagged release (v6.0.0-alpha.5 or later) when cut.
Credential-reject-on-401 feature is implemented on branch soph/credential-reject-on-401 but not yet pushed or PR'd.
V1 credential-reject is reset-only (no auto-retry within the same run) — retry-and-prompt for interactive UX explicitly deferred.

humanizeBytes in internal/gitproto duplicates formatBytes in internal/syncer/progress.go — consolidation deferred because syncer imports gitproto (would require moving the helper down the dependency graph).
Streaming approach against Cloudflare was confirmed working with --all-refs but only in one test run; no regression test covers the CDN idle-write scenario automatically.

go-git's Encoder has no progress hook for the delta-selection phase; progress is inferred from byte counts (<=12 bytes = still selecting). A proper upstream hook would be cleaner but was not proposed.
